narrative: A carpenter was installing drywall on the second floor of a house under construction when he fell about eight feet from a portable ladder and fractured his left wrist.
A warehouse worker slipped on a wet floor while walking to the loading dock and fractured his ankle.
A laborer lifting a heavy box of floor tiles in a warehouse strained his lower back.
While using a nail gun to frame a wall at a commercial building construction site, a worker discharged a nail into his eye.
A dockworker was struck by a falling steel beam that slipped from a crane, fracturing his shoulder.
A janitor cleaning a food-processing machine cut his hand on the machine's blade.
A forklift operator in a distribution warehouse was struck by a pallet that fell from the forklift's raised forks, fracturing his foot.
A framer on a residential construction site fell six feet from scaffolding and fractured his wrist.
A worker slipped and fell on the same level on an icy walkway outside the plant, spraining his ankle.
A roofer fell more than thirty feet to the ground when the roof structure he was standing on collapsed, fracturing multiple bones.
